frameworks.name: serial dependence
frameworks.one_line: current perception is pulled toward recently seen features

frameworks.name: continuity field
frameworks.one_line: spatiotemporal window over which prior stimuli assimilate current perception

core_question: Is serial dependence in orientation perception a stable trait within individuals, and is it tied to visual field location?
key_assumptions: serial dependence reflects a mechanism supporting perceptual stability
individual differences in serial dependence are measurable and meaningful
the continuity field has a spatial structure that can be probed by location
manipulations: test session repeated across two different days
Gabor location: foveal vs peripheral (10 deg right or left)
same-location vs changed-location measurement across days
key_findings: very high test-retest reliability of serial dependence across days within observers
within-subject consistency held only when measured at the same visual field location
consistency dropped when the location changed from day to day
interpretations: individual differences in serial dependence are stable traits across days
the continuity field varies across visual field locations in an observer-specific manner
